news.title: What made the 2026 FIFA World Cup so special?
news.lede: The pinnacle of football rose up from the fiery dunes of Qatar and flew down to North America, born again like a phoenix that comes alive every four years. The FIFA World Cup, the biggest spectacle of the planet unwrapped nearly every emotion that can be experienced through the medium of sport in another edition. Here are the moments that made this one special. Blockbusters and nail-biters [Image: MEXICO CITY, MEXICO - JUNE 11: Raul Jimenez #9 of Mexico celebrates scoring his team’s second goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group A match between Mexico and South Africa at Mexico City Stadium on June 11, 2026 in Mexico City, Mexico.

news.title: ‘Reclaim football’: Sepp Blatter joins La Liga chief in call for Gianni Infantino to resign
news.lede: Blatter attacks successor as Fifa president. Javier Tebas says system is ‘rotten at its core’. The Fifa president, Gianni Infantino, has come in for heavy criticism from his predecessor, Sepp Blatter, and the La Liga president, Javier Tebas, with the pair united in the belief that he should stand down. As Spain celebrated their World Cup win on Sunday, Tebas accused Infantino of “destroying the ­football industry” by ­prioritising the World Cup over national ­competitions.

news.title: Sepp Blatter urges football to kick out Gianni Infantino as Fifa president after World Cup ‘lost its credibility’
news.lede: Ex-Fifa president Blatter has heavily criticised his successor Infantino over controversies at the World Cup. From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging. At such a critical moment in US history, we need reporters on the ground.

news.title: ‘The scrutiny is constant’: referee Anthony Taylor retires from football
news.lede: The 47-year-old took charge of five World Cup matches. Taylor has refereed in the Premier League for 16 years. Anthony Taylor has announced his retirement from elite refereeing after a distinguished career spanning more than two decades and 831 matches. One of England’s most accomplished officials, the 47-year-old officiated 668 matches in the professional game in England.

news.title: World Cup becoming a 64-team tournament would create logistical challenges - so can Gianni Infantino's idea work?
news.lede: This year's World Cup is finally over - and after 48 teams took part, it was a long, long tournament. But just as we bid to say goodbye to the 2026 edition, this competition may be getting even bigger. FIFA president Gianni Infantino says any further expansion of the World Cup for 2030 - where six countries are set to host the tournament - ‌will be discussed now the 2026 World Cup has finished. And one solution FIFA are discussing is a 64-team tournament.

news.title: Football Daily | Here are 48 reasons to cherish the World Cup (minus the Geopolitics)
news.lede: The Geopolitics World Cup is already fading from view, replaced by a thin July gruel of pre-season friendlies and transfer tales. But there’s still time to cast another look back over the 48-team, 104-game, 308-goal leviathan that has dominated the last six weeks of our lives. Without further ado, here are 48 reasons to cherish this year’s World Cup (minus the Geopolitics). The very first game between Mexico and South Africa saw three red cards dished out, ITV showing off their studio’s Manhattan backdrop, and a duck anointed as a national hero.

news.title: The managers with the most World Cup matches ever
news.lede: Didier Deschamps has left his post as France manager with the record of most FIFA World Cup matches. The former Juventus captain spent 14 years in charge of Les Bleus, his crowning glory being the team’s World Cup win in 2018. Deschamps’ France reached the final again four years later, as well the Euro 2016 decider, but didn’t win either match, leaving one of the most star-studded sides of all time with just one trophy. Nonetheless, the 57-year-old’s longevity was impressive and his name will go down in the record books as one of the few players to win the greatest trophy in international as both a player and a manager.

news.title: Eight things we learned from Spain’s 1-0 victory over Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final
news.lede: In yet another battle of attrition for this World Cup, Spain ousted the defending champs, Argentina, 1-0 in a tense, physical, and sometimes sloppy finale. An extra time banger from Ferran Torres was the difference, but it was much deeper than a timely goal. Argentina’s mastery of referees and the clock betrayed them on the biggest stage, while Spain’s consistency was the difference. Here are some things we learned from the World Cup finale.
